title: Tripterium calabricum
intro: Tripterium calabricum, also known as Tripterigium, is a flowering plant in the family Malvaceae, native to Asia and widely distributed across the continent. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, providing potential health benefits such as pain relief, fever reduction, and immune system regulation. Bioactive constituents of Tripterium calabricum include glycosides, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Tripterium calabricum is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make traditional remedies such as teas and tinctures, often in combination with other medicinal plants.
